var_functions.query_db:5.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

    'businessid_92': ['Cosmetics & Beauty Supply', 'Dentists', 'Beauty & Spas', 'Shopping', 'Hair Salons', 'Health & Medical']
}

# Create a mapping of business_id to business_ref for joining
# business_id format: businessid_X
# business_ref format: businessref_X
id_mapping = {
    'businessid_49': 'businessref_49',
    'businessid_47': 'businessref_47',
    'businessid_88': 'businessref_88',
    'businessid_33': 'businessref_33',
    'businessid_92': 'businessref_92'
}

# Create a reverse mapping for reviews
reverse_mapping = {v: k for k, v in id_mapping.items()}

# Filter reviews for businesses that accept credit cards
filtered_reviews = []

            'rating': int(review['rating'])
        }
        filtered_reviews.append(review_data)

# Create expanded records for businesses with multiple categories

for business_id, business_ref in id_mapping.items():
    if business_id in category_map:
        categories = category_map[business_id]
        for category in categories:
            # Get reviews for this business
            business_reviews = df_reviews[df_reviews['business_id'] == business_id]
            for _, review in business_reviews.iterrows():
                expanded_records.append({

                    'category': category,
                    'rating': review['rating']
                })

# Calculate reviews per category and average ratings
category_stats = df_expanded.groupby('category').agg({

}).round(2)

category_stats.columns = ['review_count', 'avg_rating']
category_stats = category_stats.reset_index()

# Sort by review count (business count, since each business appears once per category per review)

business_count_per_category = df_expanded.groupby('category')['business_id'].nunique().reset_index()
business_count_per_category.columns = ['category', 'business_count']

# Merge with ratings

category_stats = category_stats.sort_values('business_count', ascending=False)

import json
import pandas as pd

# Load the review data from the file
with open('/tmp/tmp1v_vd6r0.json', 'r') as f:
    reviews = json.load(f)

# Load the businesses that accept credit cards
businesses_data = '''[
  {"_id": "6859a000fe8b31cd7362e2ab", "business_id": "businessid_49", "name": "Steps to Learning Montessori Preschool", "review_count": "8", "is_open": "1", "attributes": {"BusinessAcceptsCreditCards": "True", "WiFi": "u'no'", "BusinessAcceptsBitcoin": "False"}, "description": "Located at 6901 Phelps Rd in Goleta, CA, this facility offers a nurturing environment for young learners, providing a range of services in Education, Elementary Schools, Child Care & Day Care, Local Services, Preschools, and Montessori Schools."},
  {"_id": "6859a000fe8b31cd7362e2ac", "business_id": "businessid_47", "name": "Breeze Blow Dry Bar", "review_count": "81", "is_open": "0", "attributes": {"ByAppointmentOnly": "False", "BusinessAcceptsCreditCards": "True", "GoodForKids": "True", "RestaurantsPriceRange2": "2", "BikeParking": "False", "BusinessParking": "{'garage': False, 'street': False, 'validated': False, 'lot': True, 'valet': False}"}, "description": "Located at 9916 Clayton Rd in St. Louis, MO, this establishment offers a wide range of services, including Hair Salons, Beauty & Spas, Hair Stylists, Skin Care, Blow Dry/Out Services, and Makeup Artists."},
  {"_id": "6859a000fe8b31cd7362e2ad", "business_id": "businessid_88", "name": "Impact Guns", "review_count": "39", "is_open": "1", "attributes": {"BusinessParking": "{'garage': False, 'street': False, 'validated': False, 'lot': False, 'valet': False}", "GoodForKids": "True", "BusinessAcceptsCreditCards": "True", "ByAppointmentOnly": "False", "BikeParking": "True"}, "description": "Located at 11655 W Executive Dr in Boise, ID, this facility offers enthusiasts a premier destination for Gun/Rifle Ranges, Active Life."},
  {"_id": "6859a000fe8b31cd7362e2af", "business_id": "businessid_33", "name": "J&Q Nails", "review_count": "28", "is_open": "1", "attributes": {"BusinessParking": "{'garage': False, 'street': False, 'validated': False, 'lot': False, 'valet': False}", "BusinessAcceptsCreditCards": "True", "AcceptsInsurance": "False", "ByAppointmentOnly": "False", "RestaurantsPriceRange2": "2", "WheelchairAccessible": "True", "BikeParking": "False"}, "description": "Located at 9655 E US Hwy 36, Unit H in Avon, IN, this establishment offers a range of services including Nail Salons, Hair Removal, Beauty & Spas, and Waxing."},
  {"_id": "6859a000fe8b31cd7362e2b1", "business_id": "businessid_92", "name": "Luminosity", "review_count": "39", "is_open": "1", "attributes": {"ByAppointmentOnly": "False", "BusinessAcceptsCreditCards": "True", "BusinessParking": "{'garage': True, 'street': False, 'validated': False, 'lot': False, 'valet': False}", "RestaurantsPriceRange2": "2", "WheelchairAccessible": "True", "BikeParking": "True", "NoiseLevel": "u'quiet'", "WiFi": "u'no'"}, "description": "Located at 690 W Dekalb Pike in King of Prussia, PA, this business offers a diverse range of services and products in the fields of Cosmetics & Beauty Supply, Cosmetic Dentists, Tanning, Teeth Whitening, Beauty & Spas, Dentists, Shopping, Blow Dry/Out Services, Health & Medical, Spray Tanning, and Hair Salons."}
]'''

businesses = json.loads(businesses_data)

# Create a mapping of business_id to its categories
category_map = {
    'businessid_49': ['Education'],
    'businessid_47': ['Hair Salons', 'Beauty & Spas'],
    'businessid_88': ['Active Life'],
    'businessid_33': ['Hair Salons', 'Beauty & Spas'],
    'businessid_92': ['Cosmetics & Beauty Supply', 'Dentists', 'Beauty & Spas', 'Shopping', 'Hair Salons', 'Health & Medical']
}

# Create a mapping of business_id to business_ref for joining
# business_id format: businessid_X
# business_ref format: businessref_X
id_mapping = {
    'businessid_49': 'businessref_49',
    'businessid_47': 'businessref_47',
    'businessid_88': 'businessref_88',
    'businessid_33': 'businessref_33',
    'businessid_92': 'businessref_92'
}

# Create a reverse mapping for reviews
reverse_mapping = {v: k for k, v in id_mapping.items()}

# Filter reviews for businesses that accept credit cards
filtered_reviews = []
for review in reviews:
    business_ref = review['business_ref']
    if business_ref in reverse_mapping:
        business_id = reverse_mapping[business_ref]
        review_data = {
            'business_id': business_id,
            'business_ref': business_ref,
            'rating': int(review['rating'])
        }
        filtered_reviews.append(review_data)

df_reviews = pd.DataFrame(filtered_reviews)

# Create expanded records for businesses with multiple categories
expanded_records = []
for business_id, business_ref in id_mapping.items():
    if business_id in category_map:
        categories = category_map[business_id]
        for category in categories:
            # Get reviews for this business
            business_reviews = df_reviews[df_reviews['business_id'] == business_id]
            for _, review in business_reviews.iterrows():
                expanded_records.append({
                    'business_id': business_id,
                    'category': category,
                    'rating': review['rating']
                })

df_expanded = pd.DataFrame(expanded_records)

# Calculate reviews per category and average ratings
category_stats = df_expanded.groupby('category').agg({
    'rating': ['count', 'mean']
}).round(2)

category_stats.columns = ['review_count', 'avg_rating']
category_stats = category_stats.reset_index()

# Sort by review count (business count, since each business appears once per category per review)
# For business count, we need unique businesses per category
business_count_per_category = df_expanded.groupby('category')['business_id'].nunique().reset_index()
business_count_per_category.columns = ['category', 'business_count']

# Merge with ratings
category_stats = category_stats.merge(business_count_per_category, on='category')
category_stats = category_stats.sort_values('business_count', ascending=False)

print('__RESULT__:')
print(category_stats.to_json(orient='records'))

var_functions.query_db:5.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

You can go to Artesano and enjoy the massive metal sculptures made exclusively for this gallery by a single artist in South America, and you can enjoy the large hanging art as well.  Rehabbers will appreciate the meticulous dedo of this old corner on Cresson. The caterer wont be that happy as they will work out of cramped quarters on the lower level, but its not that bad.   I should say all types of parties but Bar/Bat Mitzvahs as the hands off policy is super strict even though the metal sculptures will do more harm to a thirteen year old than they can do to it, but the wall hangings and fit and finish are not that durable.

Went today for my birthday!First blowout and my hair looks hot! Makeup done as well and it also is fabulous! I didn't have to wait at all got right is and was done at the appropriate time! I will be back ...even for just a scalp massage.

Tuesday for $2 pint night is the night to come to the fox. Everything on tap counts if they have it, but they do tend to run out of some of the good stuff. Food is ok... Got the steak tacos the other night, don't expect many dressings like sour cream, guac, or a lot of flavor. They also don't give you much  salsa, but it's edible.

Pretty sure this was the best meal I've ever had in my life. And that's saying something b/c I've traveled a lot, and I take my food seriously! I'm not a big seafood fan, to be honest nothing on the menu sounded super great. So I went for it and dove straight in to the 7 course chef's tasting menu. Best decision I've ever made. Highly recommend this place for a splurge.

var_functions.query_db:5.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

var_functions.query_db:5.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.

import json
import pandas as pd

# Load reviews from the file
file_path = locals()['var_functions.query_db:16']
with open(file_path, 'r') as f:
    reviews = json.load(f)

# Businesses data from previous query
businesses_data = locals()['var_functions.query_db:34']

# Extract categories from business descriptions
def extract_categories(description):
    desc = description.lower()
    categories = []
    
    # Education
    if any(word in desc for word in ['education', 'school', 'preschool', 'montessori', 'child care']):
        categories.append('Education')
    
    # Hair Salons
    if any(word in desc for word in ['hair salon', 'hair styl', 'blow dry', 'makeup']):
        categories.append('Hair Salons')
    
    # Beauty & Spas
    if any(word in desc for word in ['beauty', 'spa', 'nail salon', 'waxing', 'skin care', 
                                    'tanning', 'teeth whitening', 'cosmetics', 'spray tanning']):
        categories.append('Beauty & Spas')
    
    # Active Life
    if any(word in desc for word in ['gun', 'rifle', 'active life']):
        categories.append('Active Life')
    
    # Dentists
    if any(word in desc for word in ['dentist', 'dental', 'teeth']):
        categories.append('Dentists')
    
    # Shopping
    if any(word in desc for word in ['shopping']):
        categories.append('Shopping')
    
    # Health & Medical
    if any(word in desc for word in ['health', 'medical']):
        categories.append('Health & Medical')
    
    # Hair Removal
    if any(word in desc for word in ['hair removal']):
        categories.append('Hair Removal')
    
    return list(set(categories))  # Remove duplicates

# Map business_id to categories and business_ref
id_mapping = {}
business_categories = {}

for business in businesses_data:
    business_id = business['business_id']
    business_num = business_id.split('_')[1]
    business_ref = f'businessref_{business_num}'
    
    id_mapping[business_id] = business_ref
    business_categories[business_id] = extract_categories(business['description'])

# Create reverse mapping
reverse_mapping = {v: k for k, v in id_mapping.items()}

# Filter reviews for these businesses
filtered_reviews = []
for review in reviews:
    business_ref = review['business_ref']
    if business_ref in reverse_mapping:
        business_id = reverse_mapping[business_ref]
        filtered_reviews.append({
            'business_id': business_id,
            'business_ref': business_ref,
            'rating': int(review['rating'])
        })

# Create expanded records for businesses with multiple categories
expanded_records = []
for review in filtered_reviews:
    business_id = review['business_id']
    if business_id in business_categories:
        categories = business_categories[business_id]
        for category in categories:
            expanded_records.append({
                'business_id': business_id,
                'category': category,
                'rating': review['rating']
            })

df_expanded = pd.DataFrame(expanded_records)

# Calculate statistics per category
category_stats = df_expanded.groupby('category').agg({
    'business_id': 'nunique',  # Count of unique businesses
    'rating': ['count', 'mean']  # Count of reviews, average rating
}).round(2)

category_stats.columns = ['business_count', 'review_count', 'avg_rating']
category_stats = category_stats.reset_index()

# Sort by business count (number of businesses) descending
category_stats = category_stats.sort_values('business_count', ascending=False)

print('__RESULT__:')
print(category_stats.to_json(orient='records'))

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

You can go to Artesano and enjoy the massive metal sculptures made exclusively for this gallery by a single artist in South America, and you can enjoy the large hanging art as well.  Rehabbers will appreciate the meticulous dedo of this old corner on Cresson.  But, for me whose foray into the purchase of original art extends to 3 encounters with minor minor artists, the attraction to Artesano is as a venue for small weddings and all sorts of parties for up to 120 guests.  The two level affair is beautiful, melding old original elements, weathered redone beams and brick with modern touches for wonderful result.  The caterer wont be that happy as they will work out of cramped quarters on the lower level, but its not that bad.   I should say all types of parties but Bar/Bat Mitzvahs as the hands off policy is super strict even though the metal sculptures will do more harm to a thirteen year old than they can do to it, but the wall hangings and fit and finish are not that durable.

val_args.code:
import json
import pandas as pd

# Load reviews from the file
file_path = locals()['var_functions.query_db:16']
with open(file_path, 'r') as f:
    reviews = json.load(f)

# Businesses data from previous query
businesses_data = locals()['var_functions.query_db:34']

# Extract categories from business descriptions
def extract_categories(description):
    desc = description.lower()
    categories = []
    
    # Education
    if any(word in desc for word in ['education', 'school', 'preschool', 'montessori', 'child care']):
        categories.append('Education')
    
    # Hair Salons
    if any(word in desc for word in ['hair salon', 'hair styl', 'blow dry', 'makeup']):
        categories.append('Hair Salons')
    
    # Beauty & Spas
    if any(word in desc for word in ['beauty', 'spa', 'nail salon', 'waxing', 'skin care', 
                                    'tanning', 'teeth whitening', 'cosmetics', 'spray tanning']):
        categories.append('Beauty & Spas')
    
    # Active Life
    if any(word in desc for word in ['gun', 'rifle', 'active life']):
        categories.append('Active Life')
    
    # Dentists
    if any(word in desc for word in ['dentist', 'dental', 'teeth']):
        categories.append('Dentists')
    
    # Shopping
    if any(word in desc for word in ['shopping']):
        categories.append('Shopping')
    
    # Health & Medical
    if any(word in desc for word in ['health', 'medical']):
        categories.append('Health & Medical')
    
    # Hair Removal
    if any(word in desc for word in ['hair removal']):
        categories.append('Hair Removal')
    
    return list(set(categories))  # Remove duplicates

# Map business_id to categories and business_ref
id_mapping = {}
business_categories = {}

for business in businesses_data:
    business_id = business['business_id']
    business_num = business_id.split('_')[1]
    business_ref = f'businessref_{business_num}'
    
    id_mapping[business_id] = business_ref
    business_categories[business_id] = extract_categories(business['description'])

# Create reverse mapping
reverse_mapping = {v: k for k, v in id_mapping.items()}

# Filter reviews for these businesses
filtered_reviews = []
for review in reviews:
    business_ref = review['business_ref']
    if business_ref in reverse_mapping:
        business_id = reverse_mapping[business_ref]
        filtered_reviews.append({
            'business_id': business_id,
            'business_ref': business_ref,
            'rating': int(review['rating'])
        })

# Create expanded records for businesses with multiple categories
expanded_records = []
for review in filtered_reviews:
    business_id = review['business_id']
    if business_id in business_categories:
        categories = business_categories[business_id]
        for category in categories:
            expanded_records.append({
                'business_id': business_id,
                'category': category,
                'rating': review['rating']
            })

df_expanded = pd.DataFrame(expanded_records)

# Calculate statistics per category
category_stats = df_expanded.groupby('category').agg({
    'business_id': 'nunique',  # Count of unique businesses
    'rating': ['count', 'mean']  # Count of reviews, average rating
}).round(2)

category_stats.columns = ['business_count', 'review_count', 'avg_rating']
category_stats = category_stats.reset_index()

# Sort by business count (number of businesses) descending
category_stats = category_stats.sort_values('business_count', ascending=False)

print('__RESULT__:')
print(category_stats.to_json(orient='records'))

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

You can go to Artesano and enjoy the massive metal sculptures made exclusively for this gallery by a single artist in South America, and you can enjoy the large hanging art as well.  Rehabbers will appreciate the meticulous dedo of this old corner on Cresson.  But, for me whose foray into the purchase of original art extends to 3 encounters with minor minor artists, the attraction to Artesano is as a venue for small weddings and all sorts of parties for up to 120 guests.  The two level affair is beautiful, melding old original elements, weathered redone beams and brick with modern touches for wonderful result.  The caterer wont be that happy as they will work out of cramped quarters on the lower level, but its not that bad.   I should say all types of parties but Bar/Bat Mitzvahs as the hands off policy is super strict even though the metal sculptures will do more harm to a thirteen year old than they can do to it, but the wall hangings and fit and finish are not that durable.
